name: python-barcode

metadata.name: python-barcode
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Create standard barcodes with Python. No external modules needed. (optional Pillow support included).

documentation:
# python-barcode

```{image} https://action-badges.now.sh/WhyNotHugo/python-barcode
:alt: CI status
:target: https://github.com/WhyNotHugo/python-barcode/actions
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/python-barcode/badge/
:alt: documentation
:target: https://python-barcode.rtfd.org/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/python-barcode.svg
:alt: version on pypi
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/python-barcode
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/python-barcode.svg
:alt: downloads
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/python-barcode
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/python-barcode.svg
:alt: licence
:target: https://github.com/WhyNotHugo/python-barcode/blob/main/LICENCE
```

**python-barcode** provides a simple way to create barcodes in Python.

There are no external dependencies when generating SVG files.
Pillow is required for generating images (e.g.: PNGs).

Support Python 3.7 to 3.11.

```{image} example-ean13.png
:alt: python-barcode
:target: https://github.com/WhyNotHugo/python-barcode
```

## Documentation

Full documentation is published at <http://python-barcode.rtfd.io/>

## Licence

python-barcode is licensed under the MIT licence. See LICENCE for details.

classes.name: barcode.errors.IllegalCharacterError
documentation: Raised when a barcode-string contains illegal characters.

classes.name: barcode.errors.BarcodeNotFoundError
documentation: Raised when an unknown barcode is requested.

classes.name: barcode.errors.NumberOfDigitsError
documentation: Raised when the number of digits do not match.

classes.name: barcode.errors.WrongCountryCodeError
documentation:
Raised when a JAN (Japan Article Number) don't starts with 450-459
or 490-499.

modules.name: barcode.codabar
documentation:
Module: barcode.codabar

:Provided barcodes: Codabar (NW-7)

classes.name: barcode.codabar.CODABAR
documentation:
Initializes a new CODABAR instance.

:parameters:
    code : String
        Codabar (NW-7) string that matches [ABCD][0-9$:/.+-]+[ABCD]
    writer : barcode.writer Instance
        The writer to render the barcode (default: SVGWriter).
    narrow: Integer
        Width of the narrow elements (default: 2)
    wide: Integer
        Width of the wide elements (default: 5)
        wide/narrow must be in the range 2..3

modules.name: barcode.itf

Module: barcode.itf

:Provided barcodes: Interleaved 2 of 5

classes.name: barcode.itf.ITF
documentation:
Initializes a new ITF instance.

:parameters:
    code : String
        ITF (Interleaved 2 of 5) numeric string
    writer : barcode.writer Instance
        The writer to render the barcode (default: SVGWriter).
    narrow: Integer
        Width of the narrow elements (default: 2)
    wide: Integer
        Width of the wide elements (default: 5)
        wide/narrow must be in the range 2..3

w = wide space

w = wide space

modules.name: barcode
documentation:
This package provides a simple way to create standard barcodes.
It needs no external packages to be installed, the barcodes are
created as SVG objects. If Pillow is installed, the barcodes can also be
rendered as images (all formats supported by Pillow).

functions.name: barcode.get
documentation:
Helper method for getting a generator or even a generated code.

```
param name

The name of the type of barcode desired.

param code

The actual information to encode. If this parameter is
provided, a generated barcode is returned. Otherwise, the barcode class
is returned.

param Writer writer

An alternative writer to use when generating the
barcode.

param options

Additional options to be passed on to the barcode when
generating.
```

functions.name: barcode.generate
documentation:
Shortcut to generate a barcode in one line.

```
param name

Name of the type of barcode to use.

param code

Data to encode into the barcode.

param writer

A writer to use (e.g.: ImageWriter or SVGWriter).

param output

Destination file-like or path-like where to save the generated
barcode.

param writer_options

Options to pass on to the writer instance.

param text

Text to render under the barcode.
```

modules.name: barcode.isxn
documentation:
Module: barcode.isxn

```
Provided barcodes

ISBN-13, ISBN-10, ISSN
```

This module provides some special codes, which are no standalone barcodes.
All codes where transformed to EAN-13 barcodes. In every case, the checksum
is new calculated.

Example:

```
>>> from barcode import get_barcode
>>> ISBN = get_barcode('isbn10')
>>> isbn = ISBN('0132354187')
>>> isbn
'0132354187'
>>> isbn.get_fullcode()
'9780132354189'
>>> # Test with wrong checksum
>>> isbn = ISBN('0132354180')
>>> isbn
'0132354187'
```

classes.name: barcode.isxn.InternationalStandardBookNumber13
documentation:
Initializes new ISBN-13 barcode.

:parameters:
    isbn : String
        The isbn number as string.
    writer : barcode.writer Instance
        The writer to render the barcode (default: SVGWriter).

classes.name: barcode.isxn.InternationalStandardBookNumber10
documentation:
Initializes new ISBN-10 barcode. This code is rendered as EAN-13 by
prefixing it with 978.

:parameters:
    isbn : String
        The isbn number as string.
    writer : barcode.writer Instance
        The writer to render the barcode (default: SVGWriter).

classes.name: barcode.isxn.InternationalStandardSerialNumber
documentation:
Initializes new ISSN barcode. This code is rendered as EAN-13
by prefixing it with 977 and adding 00 between code and checksum.

:parameters:
    issn : String
        The issn number as string.
    writer : barcode.writer Instance
        The writer to render the barcode (default: SVGWriter).

classes.name: barcode.codex.Gs1_128
documentation:
following the norm, a gs1-128 barcode is a subset of code 128 barcode,
it can be generated by prepending the code with the FNC1 character
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GS1-128
https://www.gs1-128.info/


documentation:
Saves the rendered output to `filename`.

```
parameters

filename

String

Filename without extension.

output

String

The rendered output.

```

methods.name: barcode.writer.BaseWriter.register_callback
documentation:
Register one of the three callbacks if not given at instance
creation.

:parameters:
    action : String
        One of 'initialize', 'paint_module', 'paint_text', 'finish'.
    callback : Function
        The callback function for the given action.

methods.name: barcode.writer.BaseWriter.set_options
documentation:
Sets the given options as instance attributes (only
if they are known).

```
parameters

options

Dict

All known instance attributes and more if the childclass
has defined them before this call.

rtype

None
```

methods.name: barcode.writer.BaseWriter.packed
documentation:
Pack line to list give better gfx result, otherwise in can
result in aliasing gaps
'11010111' -> [2, -1, 1, -1, 3]

This method will yield a sequence of pairs (width, height_factor).

:parameters:
    line: String
        A string matching the writer spec
        (only contain 0 or 1 or G).

methods.name: barcode.writer.BaseWriter.render
documentation:
Renders the barcode to whatever the inheriting writer provides,
using the registered callbacks.

:parameters:
    code : List
        List of strings matching the writer spec
        (only contain 0 or 1 or G).

Baseclass for all writers.

Initializes the basic writer options. Childclasses can add more
attributes and can set them directly or using
`self.set_options(option=value)`.

:parameters:
    initialize : Function
        Callback for initializing the inheriting writer.
        Is called: `callback_initialize(raw_code)`
    paint_module : Function
        Callback for painting one barcode module.
        Is called: `callback_paint_module(xpos, ypos, width, color)`
    paint_text : Function
        Callback for painting the text under the barcode.
        Is called: `callback_paint_text(xpos, ypos)` using `self.text`
        as text.
    finish : Function
        Callback for doing something with the completely rendered
        output.
        Is called: `return callback_finish()` and must return the
        rendered output.

methods.name: barcode.writer.SVGWriter.write
documentation:
Write `content` into a file-like object.

Content should be a barcode rendered by this writer.

methods.name: barcode.writer.ImageWriter.__init__
documentation:
Initialise a new write instance.

:params format: The file format for the generated image. This parameter can
    take any value that Pillow accepts.
:params mode: The colour-mode for the generated image. Set this to RGBA if
    you wish to use colours with transparency.

methods.name: barcode.writer.ImageWriter.write
documentation:
Write `content` into a file-like object.

Content should be a barcode rendered by this writer.
